text: The laparoscopic view reveals a subhepatic appendix formiformis, identified during exploration following a cholecystectomy procedure. The appendix is atypically located near the liver, prompting consideration for simultaneous laparoscopic appendectomy.
The surgeon grasps the appendix and suspends it to expose the mesoappendix. The mesoappendix is then dissected using ligature, separating it from surrounding tissues.
The surgeon ligates the arteria appendicularis using a plastic polymer endoclip and then cuts it with a ligature, securing the blood vessel during the appendectomy procedure.

The surgeon closes the stump of the appendix with two plastic polymer endoclips and transects the appendix using scissors. The appendix is then placed in a specimen bag and removed from the abdominal cavity.
